questions.question: Who is the author of the story ‘The Necklace’?
questions.answer: The story ‘The Necklace’ is written by Guy de Maupassant.

questions.question: Why is Mme Loisel unhappy at the beginning of the story?
questions.answer: Mme Loisel is unhappy because she is dissatisfied with her simple life and desires luxury.

questions.question: What is Mr Loisel’s occupation?
questions.answer: Mr Loisel works as a clerk in an office.

questions.question: What invitation do the Loisels receive?
questions.answer: They receive an invitation to attend a minister’s ball.

questions.question: Why does Mme Loisel feel upset after receiving the invitation?
questions.answer: She feels upset because she does not have a suitable dress to wear.

questions.question: How does Mr Loisel help his wife prepare for the ball?
questions.answer: He gives her money to buy a new dress.

questions.question: From whom does Mme Loisel borrow the necklace?
questions.answer: Mme Loisel borrows the necklace from her friend Mme Forestier.

questions.question: How does Mme Loisel feel at the ball?
questions.answer: She feels happy, proud, and admired by everyone.

questions.question: What happens to the necklace after the ball?
questions.answer: The necklace is lost after the ball.

questions.question: What decision do the Loisels take after losing the necklace?
questions.answer: They decide to replace the lost necklace.

questions.question: How do the Loisels arrange money to buy the new necklace?
questions.answer: They borrow money from different sources.

questions.question: For how many years do the Loisels suffer to repay the debt?
questions.answer: They suffer for ten years to repay the debt.

questions.question: What kind of life does Mme Loisel lead after the loss of the necklace?
questions.answer: She leads a life of poverty and hard work.

questions.question: What change is seen in Mme Loisel after ten years?
questions.answer: She becomes strong, mature, and hardened by life.

questions.question: Whom does Mme Loisel meet at the end of the story?
questions.answer: She meets Mme Forestier at the end of the story.

questions.question: What shocking truth is revealed at the end?
questions.answer: The necklace was fake and not valuable.

questions.question: Which literary device is prominently used in the ending?
questions.answer: Irony is prominently used in the ending.

questions.question: What is the main theme of the story?
questions.answer: The main theme is appearance versus reality.

questions.question: Which quality of Mr Loisel is highlighted in the story?
questions.answer: His patience and sacrifice are highlighted.

questions.question: Why does Mme Loisel not tell the truth immediately?
questions.answer: She fears humiliation and shame.

questions.question: What lesson does the story teach?
questions.answer: The story teaches the importance of honesty and contentment.

questions.question: What does the necklace symbolise?
questions.answer: The necklace symbolises false pride and appearance.

questions.question: What type of story is ‘The Necklace’?
questions.answer: It is a realistic short story.

questions.question: What causes the suffering of the Loisels?
questions.answer: Pride and dishonesty cause the suffering of the Loisels.

questions.question: Why is the title ‘The Necklace’ appropriate?
questions.answer: The necklace is central to the plot and causes the major turning point.
